title: Fearfully and Wonderfully Made
reading:
Before a word was spoken over you by anyone else — parent, teacher, friend, critic — God spoke over you. Genesis 1:26: Let Us make man in Our image.

You are not an accident. You are not a product of circumstance. You were crafted in the image of the living God. That phrase carries more weight than most people ever stop to receive. To bear God's image is to carry something of His nature into the world. It means your existence has inherent dignity — not because of what you do, but because of who made you.

David understood this. Psalm 139:14 — I am fearfully and wonderfully made. This wasn't arrogance. It was agreement. David looked at himself through God's eyes and said: Your works are marvelous, and I am one of them.

People pleasing begins in part when we forget this. When we believe that the opinions of others can define us more accurately than the Word of the one who made us. But your Maker does not regret His work. He does not wish He had made you differently. He called His creation very good — and that includes you.
reflection: Do you see yourself the way God sees you? What would change if you genuinely believed you were fearfully and wonderfully made?
abide: Father, I receive what You declared over me. I am Your work, and Your work is good. Teach me to see myself through Your eyes.
